title: DOME Registry User Schema
description: JSON Schema document describing the structure of a DOME Registry user account record, as stored in the MongoDB database. User records are linked to DOME Registry entries via the 'user' field in each entry (referencing the user _id). This schema is descriptive of the current v1.0.0 data model; it is not a formal validation specification. All personal identifiable information (email, name, ORCID) is held server-side and is never exposed in public API responses or exports outside of authorised administrative contexts.

_id.description: [SYSTEM] MongoDB document identifier. Generated server-side; uniquely identifies the user account. Referenced by the 'user' field in DOME Registry entry documents.

email.description: [PII] The user's email address. Used for authentication and account identification. Not exposed in public-facing API responses. Required for account creation.

name.description: [PII, Optional] The user's given name, if provided. Present for approximately 50% of accounts.

surname.description: [PII, Optional] The user's family name, if provided. Sparsely populated.

orcid.description: [Optional] The user's ORCID iD in 16-digit grouped format (e.g. '0000-0002-1825-0097'). Present for approximately 90% of accounts. Used to link Registry contributions to a persistent researcher identifier.

roles.description: The user's access role within the Registry. Controls which actions (view, edit, admin) the user can perform.

organisation.description: [Legacy] Free-text organisation name field. Largely superseded by the 'organizations' array. Most existing records carry the value 'undefined' in this field.

organizations.description: Array of organisation or sector affiliations for the user. These are used for Registry analytics and user segmentation. Not a free-text field — values should match the controlled vocabulary used by the Registry.
